generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components //
instance AxisDriveMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (PMSM) integrated with gear transmission for torque/speed management in the MABI MAX 100's articulated axis system, enabling high-precision motion control with closed-loop feedback capabilities."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gear transmission component integrated with each axis drive motor to manage torque/speed trade-offs, providing high torque at low speeds while minimizing backlash for precise motion control."
]

instance SINUMERIK840DslController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl motion control system responsible for trajectory planning, servo control, and real-time kinematic calculations across all six articulated axes of the MABI MAX 100 robot."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance AxisPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution position encoder embedded in each articulated axis of the MABI MAX 100, providing real-time positional feedback for closed-loop control with sub-millimeter accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AxisPositionData
]

instance AxisForceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Force/torque sensor integrated into each axis of the MABI MAX 100 to monitor dynamic load conditions, enabling adaptive control and predictive maintenance capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AxisForceData
]

instance AxisVelocityEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Velocity encoder paired with each axis position encoder to provide real-time velocity feedback for dynamic motion control and trajectory optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AxisVelocityData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance AxisPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AxisPositionEncoder
]

instance AxisForceData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AxisForceSensor
]

instance AxisVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AxisVelocityEncoder
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance KinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "6-axis Denavit-Hartenberg parameterized kinematic model implemented in Simulink for forward/inverse kinematics calculations, enabling precise motion planning and trajectory generation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkPhysicsEngine
]

instance DynamicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Newton-Euler based rigid-body dynamic model integrated with Simulink for real-time simulation of axis forces, accelerations, and energy consumption under varying load conditions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkPhysicsEngine
]

instance ControlAlgorithmModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "PID/feedforward control algorithm model implemented in C++ for low-latency axis motion control, integrated with the SINUMERIK 840D sl system for real-time execution."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlAlgorithmEngine
]

instance AxisPositionDataAggregated : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated time-series positional data from all six articulated axes, processed into standardized KPIs (e.g., positional accuracy metrics, motion profiles) for analysis and visualization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AxisPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalShadowAggregator
]

instance AxisForceDataAggregated : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed force/torque data from all axes, normalized and aggregated into contextual traces for vibration analysis, load monitoring, and predictive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AxisForceData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalShadowAggregator
]

instance AxisVelocityDataAggregated : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Velocity data from all axes aggregated into motion profiles, enabling analysis of acceleration patterns, speed variations, and dynamic load responses."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AxisVelocityData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalShadowAggregator
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SimulinkPhysicsEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment hosting real-time physics models for kinematic and dynamic simulations, enabling bidirectional validation between digital and physical systems."
    DTDFVocab:enables KinematicSimulationService, DynamicSimulationService
]

instance ControlAlgorithmEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Low-latency C++ control algorithm engine running on industrial-grade hardware, executing PID/feedforward control logic for real-time axis motion correction."
    DTDFVocab:enables MotionControlService, AdaptiveControlService
]

instance DigitalShadowAggregator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Apache Kafka-based data aggregation pipeline implementing Kritzinger's Digital Shadow framework, processing raw sensor data into structured contextual traces for analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalShadowService
]

instance MontiArcAutomaton : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc architecture description tool modeling robot state transitions and component interactions using automata theory, enabling logical architecture validation."
    DTDFVocab:enables StateTransitionService
]

instance OPCUACommunicationLayer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "OPC UA-based industrial communication layer enabling standardized information exchange between physical assets, digital twins, and higher-level MES systems."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance EdgeGateway : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Siemens SCALANCE industrial edge gateway processing sensor data locally to reduce cloud latency while maintaining real-time capabilities for control and monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:enables LocalDataProcessingService
]

// C6: Services
instance KinematicSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time kinematic simulation service providing forward/inverse kinematics calculations and trajectory validation for the 6-axis articulated robot."
    DTDFVocab:provides KinematicValidationInsight
]

instance DynamicSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic simulation service modeling rigid-body physics for all axes under varying load conditions, enabling predictive analysis of motion stability."
    DTDFVocab:provides DynamicBehaviorInsight
]

instance MotionControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time motion control service executing PID/feedforward algorithms to correct axis positions, velocities, and torques based on digital twin predictions."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionCorrectionAction
]

instance AdaptiveControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Adaptive control service dynamically adjusting control parameters based on real-time sensor data and digital twin predictions to optimize performance under varying loads."
    DTDFVocab:provides AdaptiveControlAdjustmentAction
]

instance DigitalShadowService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Passive digital shadow service aggregating and abstracting raw sensor data into contextual traces for performance analysis, fault detection, and historical comparison."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalDataInsight
]

instance StateTransitionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Robot state transition service modeling and validating motion states (idle, moving, error) using MontiArc automata for logical architecture consistency checks."
    DTDFVocab:provides StateConsistencyInsight
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "OPC UA-based real-time monitoring service providing live access to all axis states, sensor readings, and system health metrics via industrial information models."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemHealthInsight
]

instance LocalDataProcessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Edge-based data processing service performing initial feature extraction and anomaly detection on sensor data before cloud transmission, reducing latency."
    DTDFVocab:provides EdgeProcessingInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance KinematicValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results comparing simulated kinematic trajectories with actual physical motion, identifying positional errors and recommending trajectory corrections."
]

instance DynamicBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of dynamic system behavior under varying loads, identifying resonance frequencies, stability margins, and potential failure modes."
]

instance MotionCorrectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time correction commands sent to the SINUMERIK 840D sl controller to adjust axis positions, velocities, and torques based on digital twin predictions."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AdaptiveControlAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of control parameters (PID gains, feedforward coefficients) to optimize performance under changing operational conditions."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OperationalDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Aggregated operational data traces enabling performance benchmarking, fault detection, and historical comparison of robot operations across different tasks."
]

instance StateConsistencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results ensuring logical consistency between robot states (idle/moving/error) and physical system states as modeled in MontiArc automata."
]

instance SystemHealthInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time health metrics including sensor readings, vibration levels, temperature profiles, and predicted remaining useful life for all system components."
]

instance EdgeProcessingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Results from edge-based feature extraction identifying potential anomalies in sensor data before cloud transmission, enabling early fault detection."
]

// C1: Environment
instance ManufacturingCellEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Industrial manufacturing cell housing the MABI MAX 100 serial robot with applications in assembly, welding, or material handling, characterized by high-speed motion cycles and closed-loop control requirements."
    base:contains SixAxisArticulatedRobot, Axis1BaseMounted
]

// C5: Components
instance SixAxisArticulatedRobot : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "MABI MAX 100 six-axis articulated robot system comprising six interconnected axes with PMSM drives, cycloidal gearboxes, and high-precision sensors for industrial automation applications."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingCellEnvironment
]

instance Axis1BaseMounted : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "First articulated axis of the MABI MAX 100 specifically instrumented for digital shadow creation, featuring bidirectional data flow between physical and digital representations."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingCellEnvironment
    base:contains AxisDriveAssembly
]

instance AxisDriveAssembly : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Hierarchical mechanical assembly combining one PMSM motor with one cycloidal gearbox to form a single articulated axis with precise torque/speed characteristics."
    base:isContainedIn Axis1BaseMounted
    base:contains AxisDriveMotor, CycloidalGearbox
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates with real-time synchronization between physical and digital components, leveraging time-series data streams of all sensors transmitted via **MQTT** for continuous monitoring. The temporal granularity is defined by the frequency of sensor updates, which are processed to reflect the physical system's state with minimal latency. While specific synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz or ms intervals) are not detailed, the system relies on **streaming protocols** to ensure near-real-time alignment between the physical and digital representations. Behavior modeling integrates with simulation tools like **MathWorks Simulink** or other simulation methods to validate temporal consistency across functional and logical architectures (e.g., RFLP layers). Latency requirements are implicitly addressed through the use of active components in the Digital Twin to process and push data back to the physical system, ensuring responsive interactions within the defined time scales."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is orchestrated using a **layered, hierarchical architecture** derived from **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies like **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)** and **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **SysML IBDs (Internal Block Diagrams)**, **AADL (Architecture Analysis & Design Language)**, or **MontiArc**. The system integrates **component-based design**, where components and connectors are explicitly modeled to describe **structural and behavioral relationships**. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Kritzinger et al.) is referenced as a foundational concept, emphasizing **active components** in Digital Twins to process and push data back to physical systems, unlike passive Digital Shadows. The system may employ **state machines** (e.g., in SysML v1.7) or **automata** (e.g., MontiArc Automaton) for behavioral coordination. However, no explicit mention of **service-oriented architectures**, **microservices**, **event-driven mechanisms**, or **resource management strategies** (e.g., load balancing, failover) is provided. Integration of tools like **Simulink** or **general-purpose languages (C++, Java)** suggests a **heterogeneous toolchain** for behavioral modeling but does not detail system-wide orchestration patterns."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which decompose system representation into hierarchical abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. Transition management between these stages is facilitated by **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which define components, interfaces, connectors, and composition rules. Behavior modeling at the logical and product layers integrates **state machines (SysML v1.7), MontiArc Automaton, or simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, general-purpose languages like C++/Java). Lifecycle governance involves **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** to align perspectives across modeling notations and tools, ensuring consistency via **version control** (implicitly referenced for evolving DT representations). The lifecycle evolves from **passive data aggregation (digital shadow)** to **active DT functionality**, where the DT acts as an autonomous entity capable of processing and feeding insights back to the physical system. No explicit mention of formal governance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC standards) is provided, but the reliance on **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) implies adherence to structured engineering practices.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system validates model fidelity through **comparative analysis of simulated vs. measured data**, ensuring alignment between digital and physical states. Validation employs **robust, real-time identification methods** for both **white-box and black-box friction models**, addressing dynamic system behaviors. **Traceability mechanisms** are implemented to link data across modeling tools, enabling meaningful cross-verification. Uncertainty quantification and error correction mechanisms are implied but not explicitly detailed in the provided context. Quality assurance relies on **multi-layered MBSE validation** (requirements → functional → physical layers) and **simulation-based verification** to maintain consistency and accuracy."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The document does not provide explicit details on the technical network infrastructure for Digital Twin systems. However, it mentions that digital shadows (which the system aligns with) rely on **MQTT** for data transmission from physical objects to digital representations. No information is provided regarding network topology, bandwidth requirements, latency specifications, redundancy mechanisms, or security protocols for network communication. The document focuses on modeling methodologies and passive data aggregation rather than active control or network architecture."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with **external systems** primarily through **MQTT-based streaming protocols** for sensor data acquisition, enabling real-time data exchange with physical components. However, the documents do **not explicitly mention interoperability standards (e.g., OPC UA, DDS)** or integration patterns with **enterprise systems** (e.g., ERP, MES). While **heterogeneous stakeholder perspectives** are acknowledged as a challenge in MBSE (e.g., integration of modeling tools like SysML, Simulink, or general-purpose code), no specific details are provided on **cross-system dependencies** or **Digital Twin-to-Digital Twin coordination**. The focus remains on **internal modeling frameworks** (ADLs, simulation tools) rather than external ecosystem integration."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) system adheres to **industry standards and specifications** primarily through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** frameworks and **architecture description languages (ADLs)**. Key compliance elements include:
- **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)**: Used for **functional and logical architecture modeling**, including **Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)** and **state machines (v1.7)** to describe components, interfaces, and behavior.
- **AADL (Architecture Analysis & Design Language)**: Employed for **component and connector specifications**, enabling detailed logical architecture descriptions.
- **MontiArc**: Supports **automaton-based behavioral modeling** for dynamic system analysis.
- **Interoperability standards**: While not explicitly named, the system implies compliance with **OMG (Object Management Group) standards** (e.g., SysML, AADL) to ensure toolchain integration (e.g., SysML + Simulink). **Certification requirements** or **formal compliance frameworks** (e.g., ISO/IEC standards) are not mentioned in the documents.

The text does not reference **specific standards like IEEE 1735, SAE AS5506C, or DT-specific frameworks** (e.g., ISO 23247), focusing instead on **MBSE methodologies and ADLs** as foundational compliance mechanisms.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents incorporates **physical safety mechanisms** through the implementation of a **safety cell** for robotic systems (e.g., the MABI robotic arm), which restricts the maximum working range to **[-90°, 90°]** to prevent collisions or unsafe operations. This is explicitly documented in the context of robotic motion control (e.g., [44] MABI robot documentation).

For **data streaming and communication**, the system utilizes **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)** as the protocol for transmitting real-time sensor data and operational states (e.g., [52] Light, 2017). While MQTT itself does not inherently enforce security, the documents imply reliance on **secure communication channels** for data transmission, though no explicit mention of encryption (e.g., TLS), authentication (e.g., X.509 certificates), or access control mechanisms is provided.

No documentation exists regarding **cybersecurity protocols**, **risk assessment frameworks**, **fail-safe mechanisms**, or **compliance with safety standards** (e.g., IEC 62443, ISO 27001). The provided sources focus on **model-driven engineering**, **digital shadow integration**, and **friction modeling** ([43], [45]) but do not address digital security, data governance, or safety-critical system considerations beyond physical constraints.
"
]
